name: ExtensionMeasureReport_InputParameters
title: R5: What parameters were provided to the report (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `MeasureReport.inputParameters` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`MeasureReport.inputParameters` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Parameters)`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `MeasureReport.inputParameters` 0..1 `Reference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/Parameters)`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `MeasureReport.inputParameters` has a context of MeasureReport based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MeasureReport`.
Element `MeasureReport.inputParameters` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: What parameters were provided to the report (new)
element.comment:
Element `MeasureReport.inputParameters` has a context of MeasureReport based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `MeasureReport`.
Element `MeasureReport.inputParameters` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
Whenever an operation returns a MeasureReport, the IN parameters from that operation can be returned to the caller of the operation using MeasureReport.inputParameters. Operations include but are not limited to $evaluate-measure, $care-gaps, $data-requirements, and $collect-data. The inputParameters can also be used in a MeasureReport used to define a test case (i.e., not as a result of an operation).

element.short: What parameters were provided to the report
element.definition: A reference to a Parameters resource (typically represented using a contained resource) that represents any input parameters that were provided to the operation that generated the report.
element.comment: Whenever an operation returns a MeasureReport, the IN parameters from that operation can be returned to the caller of the operation using MeasureReport.inputParameters. Operations include but are not limited to $evaluate-measure, $care-gaps, $data-requirements, and $collect-data. The inputParameters can also be used in a MeasureReport used to define a test case (i.e., not as a result of an operation).
